title: MD
biography: William Osler is often called the 'Father of Modern Medicine.' A Canadian physician who established the residency system for training doctors, brought medical students to the bedside, and wrote 'The Principles and Practice of Medicine,' one of the most influential medical textbooks ever published.
aiSummary: William Osler was the Father of Modern Medicine who established the residency system and bedside teaching at Johns Hopkins. His textbook shaped medical education worldwide. Based in Baltimore, United States.

timeline.title: Medical degree from McGill University
timeline.description: Graduated and began a career that would transform medical education

timeline.title: Appointed Physician-in-Chief at Johns Hopkins
timeline.description: Helped establish Johns Hopkins Hospital as the model for modern academic medicine

timeline.title: Published 'The Principles and Practice of Medicine'
timeline.description: Wrote the definitive medical textbook used worldwide for decades

timeline.title: Regius Professor of Medicine at Oxford
timeline.description: Moved to Oxford where he continued to influence global medical education
